zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Yelverton於1618年出生在英國諾福爾可盧加姆時，她的父親William Yelverton28歲，母親Ursula Richardson21歲。她於1634年與Thomas Peyton在英國諾福爾可盧加姆結婚。他們至少是11個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1668年6月3日在她的家鄉享年50歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可布拉可 艾什。

nameMeanings.name: Yelverton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Devon): habitational name from either of two places called Yelverton, in Norfolk or Devon. Medieval examples of the surname are from the Norfolk place, but the modern surname in Britain is associated mainly with Devon, where the earliest record of the surname is found in 1761, and is probably a substitution for Elford , an earlier form of the Devon placename. The Devon place, a hamlet in Buckland Monachorum, is not officially on record as Yelverton until the 19th century, when the Great Western Railway so named the station it built there in 1859. It is a dialect pronunciation of Elverton, which is first recorded in 1765 as an extended form of its medieval name Elford + -ton ‘town’. The Norfolk placename is recorded as Ailuertuna in 1086, and probably derives from the Old English personal name Æthelfrith or Geldfrith + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Elford
meanings: English: habitational name from Yelverton in Buckland Monachorum (Devon), recorded as Elleford in 1291 (see Yelverton ), named from the Old English personal name Ella or elle, ellen ‘elder tree’ + ford ‘ford’. The name may also derive from any of the places called Elford in Staffordshire and Northumberland, both probably from the Old English personal name Ella + ford.

variants.name: Yelvington
meanings: Probably an altered form of English   Yelverton  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Yelverton was born in 1618, in Rougham, Norfolk, England, her father, William Yelverton, was 28 and her mother, Ursula Richardson, was 21. She married Thomas Peyton in 1634, in Rougham, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 11 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 3 June 1668, in her hometown, at the age of 50, and was buried in Bracon Ash, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Thomas Peyton en 1634, à Rougham, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 11 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 3 juin 1668, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterrée à Bracon Ash, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Yelvertonが1618年にイギリスノーフォルクロウアムで生まれた時，父William Yelvertonは28歳，母Ursula Richardsonは21歳であった。1634年にイギリスノーフォルクロウアムでThomas Peytonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも11人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1668年6月3日に故郷で50歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーフォルクブラコン アッシュにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Yelverton(이)가 1618년 영국 노르폴크 루그함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Yelverton(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ursula Richardson(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1634년 영국 노르폴크 루그함에서 Thomas Peyton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1668년 6월 3일 그녀의 고향에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 브라콘 애시에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Yelverton родилась в 1618 г. в местности роугам, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени William Yelverton было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Ursula Richardson, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Peyton в 1634 г. в местности роугам, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 11 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла 3 июня 1668 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 50 лет и была похоронена в местности бракон аш, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.
